question: [File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/block_service.go -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: Block()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a BlockRequest with a crafted BlockIdentifier containing a mismatched index and hash (pointing to two different real blocks) to cause RetrieveBlock() to return a block whose ConsensusStartNanos/ConsensusEndNanos belong to a different block than the one identified by hash, effectively serving transactions from a tampered historical range?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/block_service.go -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: Block()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a BlockRequest with only a hash (nil Index) to force RetrieveBlock() into the FindByHash() path, and then supply a hash that collides with or is a prefix-stripped variant of a different block's hash via SafeRemoveHexPrefix(), causing the wrong block's transaction history to be returned?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/block_service.go -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: Block()] Can an unprivileged external user send a BlockRequest with both Index and Hash set to nil (empty PartialBlockIdentifier) to trigger the RetrieveLatest() fallback path, and by doing so repeatedly during a chain reorganization window, observe inconsistent Hashgraph history that misrepresents the canonical chain state?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/block_service.go -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: Block()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the maxTransactionsInBlock truncation logic (line 61-67) by requesting a block known to contain more than maxTransactionsInBlock transactions, causing some transactions to be silently moved to OtherTransactions, thereby making the block appear to have fewer operations than actually occurred in Hashgraph history?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/block_service.go -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: Block()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a BlockRequest that causes FindBetween() to be called with ConsensusStartNanos > ConsensusEndNanos (if the block repository returns a block with inverted timestamps), resulting in an empty or incorrect transaction set being returned for that block, misrepresenting Hashgraph history?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/block_service.go -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: BlockTransaction()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a BlockTransactionRequest with a crafted BlockIdentifier.Hash containing only the '0x' prefix (empty after SafeRemoveHexPrefix stripping) to cause FindByIdentifier() to match an unintended block, then retrieve a transaction from a different block's consensus window, corrupting the apparent transaction-to-block mapping in Hashgraph history?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/block_service.go -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: BlockTransaction()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a TransactionIdentifier.Hash that exists in multiple blocks (if hash uniqueness is not enforced at the repository layer) and pair it with a BlockIdentifier pointing to a different block, causing FindByHashInBlock() to return a transaction associated with the wrong block's ConsensusStartNanos/ConsensusEndNanos, misrepresenting when the transaction occurred in Hashgraph history?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/block_service.go -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: updateOperationAccountAlias()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger cache poisoning in entityCache by causing GetAccountAlias() to return a different AccountId for the same encoded int64 key (GetId()), so that subsequent block/transaction queries for other users return operations attributed to the wrong account, corrupting the historical record of who performed which operations?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/block_service.go -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: updateOperationAccountAlias()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the LRU cache eviction policy in entityCache (configured via entityCacheConfig.MaxSize) by flooding the service with requests for many distinct accountId values, evicting legitimate cached entries and forcing re-fetches that could return stale or inconsistent alias data, causing operations in historical blocks to be attributed to incorrect accounts?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/block_service.go -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: updateOperationAccountAlias()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a request that causes entityCache.Set() to store a result keyed by result.GetId() (the alias account's encoded ID) rather than the original accountId.GetId(), creating a key mismatch where future lookups for the original account miss the cache and potentially retrieve a different alias, corrupting operation account attribution in historical blocks?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/block_service.go -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: Block()] Can an unprivileged external user send a BlockRequest with a negative block index to cause FindByIndex() or FindByIdentifier() to query for a block at a negative consensus position, potentially wrapping around or matching an unintended block in the underlying database, returning fabricated Hashgraph history?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/block_service.go -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: Block()] Can an unprivileged external user send a BlockRequest with index set to math.MaxInt64 to cause integer overflow or unexpected behavior in FindByIdentifier(), potentially causing the service to return a block from a completely different position in Hashgraph history?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/block_service.go -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: Block()] Can an unprivileged external user send a BlockRequest with index=0 (genesis block) and a hash belonging to a non-genesis block to cause FindByIdentifier() to return the genesis block's transactions under a different block's identity, misrepresenting the origin of the earliest Hashgraph history?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/block_service.go -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: RetrieveBlock()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the branch logic in RetrieveBlock() (base_service.go lines 79-89) by providing a PartialBlockIdentifier with Hash='0x' (which becomes empty string after SafeRemoveHexPrefix) and a valid Index, causing FindByIdentifier() to match any block with an empty hash, returning incorrect Hashgraph history?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/block_service.go -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: Block()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the OtherTransactions list (lines 62-66) by requesting a block where transactions beyond maxTransactionsInBlock are listed only by hash without full details, then use those hashes in subsequent BlockTransaction() calls to retrieve transactions that were intentionally excluded from the main block view, bypassing the intended transaction visibility limit?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/block_service.go -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: Block()] Can an unprivileged external user send concurrent BlockRequests for the same block to race the entityCache population in updateOperationAccountAlias(), causing a time-of-check/time-of-use condition where two different AccountId values are cached for the same key, resulting in non-deterministic operation account attribution across historical block queries?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/block_service.go -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: BlockTransaction()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a BlockTransactionRequest where BlockIdentifier.Hash contains only uppercase hex characters (e.g., '0XABCDEF') to bypass SafeRemoveHexPrefix() (which only checks for lowercase '0x'), causing FindByIdentifier() to receive a hash with the '0X' prefix intact and potentially match a different block than intended?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/block_service.go -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: Block()] Can an unprivileged external user send a BlockRequest with a hash that is a valid hex string but represents a block hash with leading zeros stripped, causing SafeRemoveHexPrefix() to return a shorter hash that matches a different block in the repository, returning that block's Hashgraph history instead of the intended one?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/block_service.go -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: Block()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that RetrieveLatest() intentionally hides the latest block (to support genesis token balance logic) by sending a nil-identifier BlockRequest, causing the service to return the second-latest block as 'latest', and then use the returned block's ParentHash/ParentIndex to reconstruct a view of Hashgraph history that omits the most recent confirmed block?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/block_service.go -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: Block()] Can an unprivileged external user send a BlockRequest with a valid block index but a hash string containing null bytes or special characters that survive SafeRemoveHexPrefix() processing, causing the repository query to behave unexpectedly and return a block with manipulated hash identity in the Hashgraph history response?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/block_service.go -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: updateOperationAccountAlias()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger a scenario where accountId.GetId() returns 0 (zero-value EncodedId) for multiple distinct accounts, causing entityCache to conflate all zero-ID accounts under a single cache entry, so that operations in historical blocks are attributed to the wrong account identity?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/block_service.go -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: Block()] Can an unprivileged external user send a high volume of BlockRequests for blocks with large transaction counts (exceeding maxTransactionsInBlock), causing the OtherTransactions slice allocation (line 62) to consume excessive memory and degrade service availability, preventing legitimate queries of Hashgraph history?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/block_service.go -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: BlockTransaction()] Can an unprivileged external user send a BlockTransactionRequest with an extremely long TransactionIdentifier.Hash string to cause FindByHashInBlock() to perform an unbounded string comparison or database query, leading to denial of service that prevents access to legitimate Hashgraph history?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/block_service.go -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: Block()] Can an unprivileged external user send a BlockRequest with a hash containing SQL injection or query injection payloads that survive SafeRemoveHexPrefix() and are passed unsanitized to FindByIdentifier() or FindByHash() in the block repository, causing the underlying database to return manipulated Hashgraph history?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/block_service.go -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: BlockTransaction()] Can an unprivileged external user send a BlockTransactionRequest with a TransactionIdentifier.Hash containing injection payloads passed to FindByHashInBlock(), causing the repository to return a transaction from outside the specified block's ConsensusStartNanos/ConsensusEndNanos window, misrepresenting Hashgraph history?
